info.title: Gamification Finances API
info.description: API para gestión de finanzas personales con gamificación

bearerAuth.description: Enter your JWT token in the format: Bearer <token>

userId.description: ID of the user who owns this transaction

id.description: Unique identifier of the income item

accountId.description: ID of the account this planned income will be deposited into. Required as of PR 1.

id.description: Unique identifier of the expense item

accountId.description: Required. Must be an account owned by the authenticated user. Response 400 if the account does not belong to this user.

incomeItems.description: Full replacement of the income list for this budget

expenseItems.description: Full replacement of the expense list for this budget

BudgetMutationResponse.description: Shape returned by bulk replace-all endpoints (PATCH /income, PATCH /expense). Includes the full enriched budget with both item lists.

data.description: Updated budget document with incomeItems[], expenseItems[], and derived totals

BudgetTotals.description: Aggregate counters recomputed after any item-level mutation.

IncomeItemMutationResponse.description: Returned by POST /income and PUT /income/:incomeId. Includes ONLY the touched item plus recomputed totals — NOT the full parent budget.

ExpenseItemMutationResponse.description: Returned by POST /expense and PUT /expense/:expenseId. Includes ONLY the touched item plus recomputed totals.

ItemRemovalResponse.description: Returned by DELETE /income/:incomeId and DELETE /expense/:expenseId. The item no longer exists, so only the recomputed totals are returned.

AuthResponse.description: Canonical auth payload. LoginResponse and RegisterResponse are name-aliases of this shape so codegen tools generate distinct named types; they remain free to diverge later without breaking the shared contract.

token.description: JWT access token. Send as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on subsequent requests.

expiresIn.description: Token lifetime in seconds from issue.

LoginResponse.description: Response shape for POST /api/auth/login. Identical to AuthResponse today; named separately to allow independent evolution.

RegisterResponse.description: Response shape for POST /api/auth/register. Identical to AuthResponse today; named separately to allow independent evolution.

UserProfile.description: Returned by GET /api/auth/me. Full profile (no password hash).

UnauthorizedError.description: Access token is missing or invalid

tags.name: Income
tags.description: Income items nested under a budget (/api/budgets/{id}/income)

tags.name: Expense
tags.description: Expense items nested under a budget (/api/budgets/{id}/expense)

post.description:
Creates a new user account and returns a JWT for immediate use — the
client does not need to call `/auth/login` after a successful register.
Email is normalized (lowercased + trimmed) by validation before storage.

409.description: A user with this email already exists

example.message: User with this email already exists

post.description:
Returns a JWT on success. **Both "user not found" and "wrong password"
intentionally return 401 with the same `INVALID_CREDENTIALS` errorCode**
to avoid leaking which emails are registered. Do not surface a
different UX for the two cases on the frontend.

401.description: Invalid credentials (user not found OR wrong password)

example.message: Account is deactivated. Please contact support.

post.description:
Adds the bearer token to a DB-backed blacklist so it cannot be reused,
even before its natural expiry. Subsequent requests with the same
token receive 401 `TOKEN_BLACKLISTED` from `authenticateJWT`.

200.description: Logged out successfully; token has been invalidated

get.description:
Returns the full profile of the user identified by the bearer token.
Use this on app startup to hydrate the auth store after a page refresh.

404.description: Authenticated user no longer exists in the database

put.description:
Updates ONLY the budget's own scalar fields. Income and expense items
**must** be managed via the dedicated nested endpoints:

- `/api/budgets/{id}/income` — add, update, or delete income items
- `/api/budgets/{id}/expense` — add, update, or delete expense items

Sending `incomeItems` or `expenseItems` in this body returns `400`.
Rationale: bulk-replacing items would delete rows referenced by
Transaction.incomeItemId / expenseItemId, breaking transaction history.

post.summary: Duplicate a budget into a new (year, month)
post.description:
Clones the source budget's `incomeItems` and `expenseItems` into a
brand-new budget at the target `year`/`month`. **Transactions are NOT
copied** — they are historical records pointing at the SOURCE items,
and the cloned items are new rows with new IDs. Copying them would
break transaction history.

Fails with:
- `400` if the target month is out of range, or if any source income
  item references an account that no longer belongs to the user
  (error message lists the offending accountIds).
- `403` if the source budget does not belong to the caller.
- `404` if the source budget does not exist.
- `409` if a budget already exists at the target `(year, month)`.

Items are NOT accepted in the body — pass only the target period.

data.description: The newly created budget with cloned incomeItems and expenseItems

403.description: Source budget does not belong to the caller

409.description: A budget already exists for the target period

get.description: Returns the paginated income items for the given budget, plus the user's accounts (useful to resolve `accountId`).

patch.summary: Replace all income items for this budget
patch.description: Overwrites the full income list. To add/update a single item, prefer POST or PUT.

patch.summary: Replace all expense items for this budget
patch.description: Overwrites the full expense list. To add/update a single item, prefer POST or PUT.

post.summary: Extract transactions from a statement image (no DB writes)
post.description:
Uploads a statement image (JPEG/PNG/WebP) and returns the extracted
transactions for review — nothing is persisted. Extraction runs behind
a swappable provider: a mock extractor by default, or Claude vision when
`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is set (and `USE_MOCK_EXTRACTION` is not `true`).
`budgetId` / `accountId` are NOT returned — the user supplies them at
confirm time. Max upload size is `MAX_UPLOAD_MB` (default 10).

sourceText.description: Raw row text as read from the image

413.description: File exceeds the upload size limit

example.message: Unsupported file type. Upload a JPEG, PNG, or WebP image.

422.description: No transactions found in the image

example.message: No transactions were found in the uploaded image.

503.description: Extraction provider unavailable (Claude selected but not configured)

post.description:
Creates the user-reviewed transactions in a single database transaction
(all-or-nothing), reusing the standard create/rebalance logic. `budgetId`
and `accountId` are batch-level; each row may override `accountId`.

accountId.description: Per-row override of the batch account

403.description: Budget or account does not belong to the user
